Off Calendar Feb-28-2023 Continued To May-23-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Houman Chitsaz appeared by zoom for Belinda Robinson (trustee).
Herman Johnson, Jr. appeared by zoom in propria persona.

Mr. Chitsaz stated that they have a tentative mediation date of April 11th, that there was a scheduling problem and they were not able to mediate within the time period that was set by the initial mediation order, and that they need another mediation order from the court. Mr. Johnson stated that he is concerned about the court ordering them to mediation by the 15th because the petitioner's attorney wasn't available for 90-days, that the attorney never contacted him about the mediation date and that the petitioner's attorney told him that he would not be forced into mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than April 25, 2023. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by May 2, 2023. The Petition for Order Confirming that an Interest in the Real Property is an Asset of the Trust, etc., status of mediation, and the case management hearing are continued to and the Parties are to return on May 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

The court notes Mr. Johnson's absence this morning. Mr. Chitsaz reports that Mr. Johnson advises him that he has not been feeling well. Mr. Chitsaz reports on the status of the matter and requests that the court continue the trial set for Apr-04-2023 as there is pending discovery and the parties are interested in mediating. Counsel's request to move the current trial date is denied without prejudice. The court confirms with counsel that all parties are amenable to participate in the court's pro bono mediation program. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed by no later than Feb-14-2023. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Feb-21-2023. The Parties are to return on Feb-28-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Petition is continued to the same date. The Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar. Counsel is instructed to file a joint case management statement prior to the next calling.

Judge: Hon. Ronald Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Yang. Not Reported.

***After Hearing (Nov-15-2022): upon further consideration, the court continues the Apr-04-2023 trial date to Jun-06-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Case Management Hearing set for Jan-31-2023 is continued to Feb-28-2023 at 9:00 am.

Judge: Hon. Ronald Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Yang. Not Reported.

The court, counsel, and Mr. Johnson discuss the submission of separate Case Management Statements (CMS) which does not comply with the court's order regarding the submission of joint CMS. The court acknowledges Mr. Johnson's objection to the submission of joint CMS and advises that it finds the objection unreasonable and urges Mr. Johson to work with counsel. Mr. Chitsaz advises that he just recently took this case and advises that he intends to work with Mr. Johnson. Mr. Johnson requests the court order the parties to mediation with Mr. Chitsaz agreeing that mediation may be helpful. The court offers its position that it doesn't appear that the matter is ready for mediation at this time and will consider the request for the next calling of this matter and encourages the parties to seek alternate means of resolution in the interim. The parties discuss service via email. The court advises the parties to meet and confer regarding that issue.

After additional discussion, the Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar and the remaining subject matter is continued to Nov-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

The court notes that it appears that Mr. Johnson's objections have standing and queries the parties regarding how they see this matter proceeding. Mr. Trowbridge advises that the parties have had informal settlement discussions and believes this matter would benefit from using the court's pro bono mediation program. Mr. Johnson disagrees stating that he doesn't believe that mediation will be useful. The court reminds the parties that regardless of their positions, they will likely be required to participate in some form of ADR prior to be set for trial. The court advises the parties that it will be issuing a Case Management Order and summarizes what will be included in the order and what the court expects from the parties. The court encourages the parties to continue to meet and confer and to be cooperative to avoid costly litigation.

The matter is continued to Aug-30-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The first Case Management Hearing is set for the same date.
